# orcasecurity_integration_slack_template (Resource)

Manages a [Slack](https://slack.com) integration in Orca Security. The integration
posts Orca alerts to one or more channels in a connected Slack workspace, and the
`mapping` controls which Orca alert fields render in the Slack message title and
description.

The Slack workspace must already be connected to Orca via the Slack app (Settings →
Integrations → Slack in the Orca UI). This resource only manages the alert-forwarding
config; it does not perform the OAuth connection.

Under the hood this creates an entry under `POST /api/external_service/config` with
`service_name = "slack"`. Updates go to
`PUT /api/external_service/config/slack?template=<template_name>`, and deletes issue a
`DELETE` for the same path.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Slack integration that posts Orca alerts to a Slack workspace/channel. The mapping
# lists which Orca alert fields render in each message section (title, description).
resource "orcasecurity_integration_slack_template" "demo" {
  template_name = "tf_slack"
  workspace_id  = "T0A0KSCQ1B3"
  channels      = ["C0AE82CGDH7"]
  show_actions  = true

  mapping = {
    title = [
      "alert_id", "orca_score", "source", "alert_labels",
      "type", "asset_type", "status", "risk_level",
    ]
    description = [
      "details", "cloud_provider", "account_name", "asset_name",
      "asset_state", "recommendation", "cve_list", "findings",
    ]
  }

  business_units = [
    "a411f20b-0276-438c-a9d5-938c48a40957",
    "930c1e5e-6b2e-4881-a393-33491e758144",
  ]

  is_enabled = true
  is_default = false
}
```

## Argument Reference

* `template_name` — (Required, String) Identifier for the integration, used as the
  URL key for updates and deletes. Changing this forces a new resource.
* `workspace_id` — (Required, String) Slack workspace (team) ID Orca posts to, e.g.
  `T0A0KSCQ1B3`. The workspace must already be connected to Orca via the Slack app.
* `channels` — (Required, List of String) Ordered list of Slack channel IDs Orca sends
  alerts to, e.g. `["C0AE82CGDH7"]`.
* `show_actions` — (Optional, Bool) Whether Slack messages include interactive action
  buttons. Default `true`.
* `mapping` — (Required, Map of List of String) Map of message section name (e.g.
  `title`, `description`) to an ordered list of Orca alert field names rendered in that
  section. Each field name is wrapped as `{"orca": "<field>"}` on the wire, so the config
  stays terse in HCL. Only Orca-field references are supported.
* `is_enabled` — (Optional, Bool) Default `true`.
* `is_default` — (Optional, Bool) Default `false`.
* `business_units` — (Optional, Set of String) Optional set of Orca business unit IDs
  that may use this integration. Orca only accepts this value at create time — changing
  the set forces Terraform to replace the integration.

## Attribute Reference

* `id` — (String) Orca external service config identifier (UUID).

## How to discover the IDs

* `workspace_id` — the Slack team ID (starts with `T`). Visible in the Slack workspace
  URL or via the Slack API `auth.test` response (`team_id`).
* `channels` — Slack channel IDs (start with `C`). In Slack, open the channel → channel
  name → **About**; the channel ID is shown at the bottom.

## Import

```bash
terraform import orcasecurity_integration_slack_template.demo tf_slack
```
